reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 4 (Exploitability): DISPUTED_FP - mitigated by dedicated resource bounds. While the code path is factually correct (RPC path at data_availability_checker.rs:247-263 performs KZG verification before filtering invalid indices, while gossip path validates index range first at data_column_verification.rs:535), this is mitigated by MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS=16384 rate limit. An attacker can send at most 16384 columns per request. With NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS=128, a malicious peer could send ~128 invalid-index columns per request to trigger KZG operations that will be filtered. However, this is bounded by the request limit and standard P2P rate limiting mechanisms, preventing resource exhaustion. The issue exists but impact is neutralized by existing request bounds. From outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:209-224, invariant text: 'sidecar.index must be in range [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS) to be accepted.' The RPC path deviates from this requirement by accepting out-of-range indices temporarily (they pass KZG verification but are filtered afterward), which is technically a spec deviation but with negligible practical impact due to request limits.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: sidecar.index must be in range [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS) to be accepted. (forall sidecar: accepted(sidecar) => sidecar.index >= 0 && sidecar.index < NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS)
